text: CFI Project
A guide to creating a database, tally sheets, and field data recorder for a new CFI project.
This User Guide was written for the Branch of Forest Inventory and Planning (FIP) for the development of a new project.
Create a new Project Folder

The Branch of Forest Inventory and Planning is responsible for developing and maintaining all CFI projects across the nation. It is important that there is consistency when a new folder is developed for a new project.
Steps for creating a new Project Folder on the T: Drive
The T: Drive, WORKGROUP, Projects is the official projects folder for all CFI Projects.
Navigate to the T:Drive, Workgroup – Projects – select the Region
T:\WORKGROUP\Projects\NorthwestRegion
Regional folders contain list of Tribes with previous inventories. If a tribe is not listed create a new folder for the unlisted tribe.
Unlisted Tribe

Copy and paste the _ReservationName within the Region folder and rename the folder with the tribe. Keep the Correspondence folder and only one of the inventory type folder. Rename the “YearContinuousForestInventory” folder with the year i.e. “2023ContinuousForestInventory”
Listed Tribe
Copy and paste the type of Inventory i.e. (YearContinuousForestInventory) from the _ReservationName folder and paste into the tribal folder then rename.
The inventory year project folder is created for the inventory for the tribe. In this example: Colville has new CFI folder for the upcoming remeasurement.
“YearContinuousForestInventory” contains 9 folders for the project

_WorkingFolder_NamePhase_Date6.11.2021 – use as a temporary Working Folder to maintain developing documents (draft field manuals, draft tatum guides, developing database, cleaning spreadsheets etc) that is currently being worked on for the Inventory Phase for the CFI. DO NOT CREATE SUBFOLDERS. Once all CFI Materials are finalized then move from the Working Folder into the appropriate folders 1_Initation to 5_ForestInventoryAnalysis – then rename the folder back to the original folder name to indicate there is NO current working phase. The folder is also used for continuity of operations – if a forester is on leave and another forester needs to assist with the project then they will check if the _WorkingFolder indicates a CFI Phase with a Date and any materials. For example, rename for your current project: _WorkingFolder_DawnInitation_7.31.2023 will indicate that the project is in the Initiation Phase.
0_Archive – use to store archive past CFI materials used to develop the current inventory. This could include past Forest Inventory Analysis (FIA), or plot design or past notes from the tribe.

1_Initation – use to store all the finalized CFI Materials for the current inventory project. Do not include the edited version of materials only the FINAL documents that was sent to the tribe to start the CFI data collection.

2_DataCollection – use to store the database received back from the tribe or region after they have completed the field data collection. It maybe necessary to store copies of updated databases if changes are made to the database during the data collection phase.
3_CleaningProcessing – use to store the cleaning spreadsheet, notes, documentation for data cleaning, and any work done for processing such as Diameter or Height regression.
4_Analysis – use to store any analysis notes, documents, files used for Analysis Runs or verification spreadsheets, or FVS databases.

5_ForestInventoryAnalysis – used to store the final database and files that will be returned to the tribe or region so they can start writing their Forest Inventory Analysis (FIA). When the tribe or region has returned the signed FIA the project is closed out.
GIS – used to store any GIS layers, maps, that was created for the project.
PlotPhotos – used to store any plot photos if the tribe or region collected for the inventory.
Steps for creating a new Project Folder on the C: drive:

Recommend using the C: Drive for the current working folder and backed up to the T: Drive during the development of the CFI Project. The reason is to increase the speed working from the C:Drive on the local laptop instead of working off the T:Drive server which can be slow to access the network. Best practices is to update the T:Drive nightly with the most currently version of your work. This will allow other team members to access your current work if they need to assist on the project.
Create a Folder on your local OSDisk (C:) example: “_ColvilleCFI2023”
Locate past CFI Documents

The Branch of Forest Inventory and Planning inventory documents are usually stored on the T:drive in the most recent inventory folder under 5_ForestInventoryAnalysis. If a CFI Inventory has been completed by the tribe the final database will be found in the “DatabaseFinalVersionforFIA” in the 5_ForestInventoryAnalysis folder and the final field manual “CFIManual” in the 1_Initation folder. Other archive documents are also stored on the S: Drive, the project lead will search the documents and copy and paste the past CFI Documents into the T: Drive Working Folder. In most cases, searching the S:drive may not be required unless research is needed for the project. Most of the documents will be found on the T:drive.
Steps for locating past CFI Documents:
Documents required for a new inventory are:
1. Field Manual from the previous inventory the final version.
2. Database from the previous inventory the final version.
3. Tatum Guides (Optional) from the previous inventory the final version.
Unlisted Tribe
There are no past field manual or database for the project. The field manual and database will be developed using the standardize CFI Materials.
Listed Tribe
Three documents are required from the previous inventory and pasted into the _WorkingFolder on the T:Drive. 1. Field Manual, Database, Tatum Guides.

If a CFI Inventory was completed for a tribe then most CFI Projects will have the final past CFI documents on the T:drive. Navigate to the Region\Tribe and find the last inventory folder. In this example Colville – the last inventory folder is “2013ContinuousForestInventory.” The “2023ContinuousForestInventory” was created in for the new inventory.
Navigate to the folder called “DatabaseFinalVersionforFIA” in the 5_ForestInventoryAnalysis folder and copy the final database into the _Working Folder.
Navigate to the folder called “CFIManual” in the 1_Initation folder and copy the final field manual into the _Working Folder.

Navigate to the folder called “TatumGuide” in the 1_Initation folder and copy the final tatum guides into the _Working Folder. In this example: there were no Tatum Guide for the 2013 Colville project.
Archived Materials

The S: Drive, BOFRP, 2_WorkGroup, BOFRP_Projects is the archive storage location of the official projects folder for all past CFI Projects. Only access the S:drive if documents cannot be found on the T:Drive under the tribal folder.
Navigate to the S:Drive and search for any documents that maybe required.
Path S:\BOFRP\2_WorkGroup\BOFRP_Projects\Northwest_Region\Colville
Search the Folder and locate the database and field manual. Ensure you select the latest database and field manual and copy these documents into the T: Drive and C: Drive Working Folders.
Standardize CFI Materials

The Branch of Forest Inventory and Planning uses TEAMS to host the location of the Standardize CFI Materials. Any update to the application, database, field manuals and CFI materials are posted to this site. The branch collaborated with the Inventory Planning Operations Group (IPOG) which included foresters from the tribe and the region to review, comment, and standardize the CFI field names and codes to streamline the CFI process. The joint effort resulted in the standardize CFI materials.
Download Standardize CFI Materials
Copy and paste the standardize blank field manual and master database into the T:drive and C:drive Working Folders. The standard CFI Materials are stored in two locations.
Navigate to the team channel - Teams\Standardize CFI Materials
1. Field Manual: _YEAR TribeCFIFieldManual DayMonthYear v.10 MASTER - TIMBER
2. CFI Database: [TribeName] CFI [Date] v.2.01.01.mdb
Navigate to the T:Drive: T:\WORKGROUP\Projects\_StandardizeCFIMaterials
Copy and paste the field manual and master database into the T:drive and C:drive Working Folders. Note: This is the backup location of the CFI Materials
Files in Working Folders on T: DRIVE and C: DRIVE
The working folder on the T:drive and C:drive should contain the previous field manual and database, and the standardize field manual and master database.

Overview of the Database Structure
The database contains tables and queries used to develop the CFI database and materials for the upcoming measurement, and process and calculate the data for the Forest Inventory Analysis.
Relationship Design

The master database is a relational database which stores and provides access to data that are related to one another. The design of the master database shows how these tables are linked and should never be deleted or replaced.
Database Tables
The tables are used to set up the initial inventory, FVS, tablet, processing, reports, store validation rules, site index, calculation tables and lookup tables.
Database Queries

The queries are action queries to generate keys for the tables, prepare the data entry screen in the application, and writing back the data to the database. There are also saved queries to review data and cross tab queries for cleaning.
CFI Project Overview (How They Are Designed)

CFI projects cross the country are similar in how the databases are developed but changes were made to streamline the process. Understanding the data from a previous database or the current database is important.
First, it is important to know how many times the CFI was measured. The ‘ProjectMeasurementPeriods’ table shows how many times the project was measured. In this example, there are eight measurements.

The ‘Plots’ and ‘Trees’ tables house the individual records for every plot and every single tree for the project. In this example: There are 1286 individual plots and 39,878 individual trees. There is no Regens table since Regen is a plot count.

The ‘PlotMeasurements’ and ‘PlotCustomMeasurements’; ‘TreeMeasurements’ and ‘TreeCustomMeasurements’; ‘RegenMeasurements’ and ‘RegenCustomMeasurements’tables are a one-to-one relationship, meaning the number of records will be identical. The Plot and tree records are for every plot and tree for every measurement. Regeneration records are different, they are the count for every measurement, and these can differ from measurement to measurement, but the total records in the tables will be identical. **Notice: Regen records do not match – further investigation needed to identify and correct.
All CFIs are established on a systematic grid on the reservation for trust acres only.

The individual plot is stored in the Plots table. Every individual tree on the plot is stored in the Trees table. The data for these table are static tables and the data should not change unless updated such as UTM Coordinates.

The measurement tables (‘PlotMeasurements’ and ‘PlotCustomMeasurements’; ‘TreeMeasurements’ and ‘TreeCustomMeasurements’; ‘RegenMeasurements’ and ‘RegenCustomMeasurements’table) are for every measurement and the data collected for each measurement can change such as DBH, etc. The table records must match. The “Measurements’ tables are static tables and the ‘CustomMeasurements’ table may vary from project to project depending on what the tribes want to collect.

The table below shows the individual records for Plots and Trees. When the records form the Plots and Trees table are multiplied by the number of measurement periods the total will equal the number of records in both the Measurements and CustomMeasurements tables.

Regeneration tables are slightly different since there is no Regens table. The regen data may be different from measurement period to measurement period and the RegenMeasurement table is a static table. The table below shows Regen records not matching. There are more records in the RegenCustomMeasurements table than the RegenMeasurements table. Always assume the RegenMeasurements table is the true count since new records are first entered into the RegenMeasurement table. The extra records in RegenCustomMeasurements may need to be deleted after further investigation.
Overview of CFI Guides

There are several guides used for reference when developing the CFI database. In addition, there are several Excel worksheets to help with the field manual formatted materials, cross walk, and validation rule lookup.
2.0 Database Field Names

The Excel document ‘Table Field Name Guide v.5 9August2023’ has the ‘2.0. db Field Names’ tab that list the standard field names for each database table that is used to develop the CFI database for data collection.

The gray fonts indicate field names that are required by the application and should never be rearranged or modified. The black fonts indicate standard field names in each table that maybe used to collect data and should never be rearranged or modified. The reason these tables should never be changed is to streamline the “Update” and “Get” queries development and the order these field names are listed is also important because they are hard coded in the application. Only use the Custom tables to add new field names for the CFI project.
Database Lookup Tables and Guides

There are 3 lookup tables in the database, AppTables, AppColumnDataTypes, and AppColumnCategoryTypes that are used when adding new field names to the Custom Tables and the AppColumn tables. There are also other lookup tables for the Tablet and FVS and those will be cover in those sections.
The Excel document ‘Table Field Name Guide v.5 9August2023’ has the ‘Apps IDs’ tab that has a quick reference guide for the look up tables.
AppTables

The ‘AppTable’ is a lookup table that identifies which table a Field Name resides or is listed and is noted in the ‘AppColumn’ table under ‘TableID’ which stores all the codes used by the application and in the data collection. New field names will only be added to the Custom tables then added to the AppColumn tables.
AppColumnDataTypes

The ‘AppColumnDataTypes’ is a lookup table that identifies the data type (i.e., Text, Integer, Date/Time) of the field name as it is entered in the respective tables (i.e., Plots, Trees) and is noted in the ‘AppColumn’ table under ‘DataTypeID’. When the field names is added to the Custom table, the Data type will be entered as either Short Text or Number, then in the Properties the Field Size will be specified as:
Field Name: Short Text – the number of characters
Field Name: Number – Integer or Long Integer

Default Value: Remove default value “0” and keep blank. When this is not left blank “0” will be added all the cells for the field name and “0” can be a valid code. MAKE SURE Default Value is left BLANK.
AppColumnCategoryTypes
The ‘AppColumnCategoryTypes’ is a lookup table that identifies the category type (i.e., Code or Range) of the field name and is noted in the ‘AppColumn’ table under ‘CategoryTypeID’.

The "Get_Queries" are critical queries used to set up the data entry screens in the DESKTOP APPLICATION. These queries are also used as translators for the FIP Apps to get and pull the data from the database; then, the "Update_Queries" writes the data back into the database through the DESKTOP APPLICATION.

Select “Queries” from the Navigation Pane

The Get Plot Measurements For Period
The Get Regen Measurements
The Get Tree Measurements For Period

Data Entry screens: The “Get Measurements Queries” are arranged in the order as they appear in the Measurement tables except for the Custom Measurement tables. Some data fields can be deleted or added to the current measurement period. After the get queries are developed, the data entry screens can be viewed in the DESKTOP APPLICATION.

AppColumn and AppColumnCodes Development

The AppColumn table is an important table and is linked to multiple tables in the database. The Field Names in the Plot/Tree/Regen tables are already listed in the AppColumn tables; however, the codes may need to be added for non-standardized field names. The AppColumnCodes table holds all the codes for the field names. The pre-work excel worksheet is very helpful to develop the AppColumn and AppColumnCodes.
Link the AppColumnCodes to AppColumn
The master database should have the tables already linked if not follow these steps to link up the AppColumnCodes to the AppColumn table.
From the Navigation Pane – open the AppColumns table.
Select the “+” to the left of the ID for “Aspect”.
Select “AppColumnCodes” in the Insert Subdatasheet dialog box, once selected the “Link Child Fields” and “Link Master Field” will auto populate.
The tables are now linked. The linked table now appears when “+” is selected.
AppColumn Table Overview
There are 10 field names that make up the AppColumn table. The only time a new name is added is when a new name is added to the CUSTOM tables and if there is no similar name in the AppColumn table.

ID – The ID guid are auto generated. When a new Column Name (aka Field Names) are added to the table a new guid is generated. Therefore, do no change these or delete since other tables refer to the guids to set up the reports and tablets.

TableID – Refers to the table that the Column Name resides. The only time a new field name should be added to the AppColumns is when a new field name is added to any of the CUSTOM tables that is not already listed in the AppColumns table. During the standardization, the team added all field names that were used nationwide. Therefore, adding a new field name should be rarely done.

DataType ID - Refers to the Data Type that was selected in the “Field Size” when the CUSTOM table was developed. The DataType ID MUST match the DataType Field Size. In this example, FLCReserve was entered into the Custom table and the Datatype was Number and the “Field Size” was Integer.

CategoryTypeID – Refers to the type of category of the field name. Most of the fields will use code “3=Code” since majority of the field name uses codes such as Species, problem code, etc. Use code “4=Range” for elevation, percent, DBH, total height and tree age range, etc. Never use Code 1 or 2.

ColumnName – This MUST match the spelling in the “Field Name” in the tables. The naming uses the Pascal naming convention such as TotalHeight, no spaces and capitalize the first letter for the word. The only time a new ColumnName is added is to the Custom tables and when there is no similar name already in the AppColumns table.

Friendly Name – Used in the tablet if the Pascal naming is hard to read. Some of the most used field names are populated. The way the field name is typed will show in the tablet when “Friendly Name” is selected.

Short Name - Used in the tablet if the Pascal naming is hard to read or the Friendly name is too long to read in the tablet. Some of the most used field names are populated. The way the field name is typed will show in the tablet when “Short Name” is selected.
Description – User preference to make notes. Some fields are populated with a description to note which regions uses the field name.
Active – Check the box to activate the ColumnName for the current. Some ColumnNames are already checked that is required by the application and the calculated tables so do not uncheck these.
StandardCode – Only the ColumnName that has standard code is checked. Do not uncheck these. The standard codes are listed for these ColumnName – to view expand the “+” to the left of the row.

Report Columns and Report Headers

The ReportHeader and ReportColumn tables are used to develop the report and error check reports. How the ReportHeader table is developed is how the Report will look in the DESKTOP APPLICATION. The ReportColumn pulls the data from the database and populates into the Report in the DESKTOP APPLICATION.
ReportHeaders Overview
The standard ReportHeaders has four columns that is used to identify which field names in the ReportColumns are used.
The column “ReportSections” identifies if the headers are for Plot, Regen, or Trees.
The column “ReportGroup” identifies the heading groups; where Plots has 3 headers, Regen has 1 header, and Tree has 1 header.
The column “LineNumber” is used to keep the headers in order for the groupings.
The Column “Header” is where the report is developed. Since this is the standard report only add custom Plot field(s) for ReportGroup 3.
Use the following “Header” to check the order of the field names.
ReportColumns Overview
The standard ReportColumns has six columns that is used to identify which field names from the AppColumns table is used in the report.
The column “ReportSections” identifies if the headers are for Plot, Regen, or Trees and should match the “ReportSection” of the “ReportHeaders” table.
The column “ColumnID” are the guids for the field names in the “AppColumns” table.
The column “ReportGroup” identifies the heading groups where the field names are listed.
The column “ReportIndex” is used to order the field names within the report.

The Column “FieldFormat” is used to move or identify the number of spaces. For example, {0,8:n0}, the number “8” and any number after the “0,” is what will be changed to move the data. {0,8:g0} or {0,8:n0}, (not sure what the g0 or n0 are used for but need it) if a new line or row is added the format of these must be in the format of the Field Format. It’s best to copy and paste these from one the Field Format.
To move the data only change the following red font numbers:
DATE,01
TEXT,50,6
The Column “TallyFormat” is not used.

Standard Validation Rules and Guides
The standard validation rules are saved in the “Standard CFI Materials/CFI Database” Teams channel. Download the “Validation and Display Rules.xlxs” to the project folder.

The excel worksheet contains 9 tabs to help with the validation rules. The ‘TOC’ tab contains all the standard field names for Plot, Tree, and Regen. The Item# listed for the field names are also used to list the errors in the “Validation List” tabs for Tree, Regen and Plots and used to reference the rule in the DESKTOP APPLICATION.

The “Tree Validation List”, “Regen Validation List” and “Plot Validation List” have the same format, the top four rows (1-4) references whether the field name meets Policy, FIA or used in the CFI Analysis or FVS. The next set of rows (6-9) refers to the field name validation and how to reference them in the DESKTOP APPLICATION (Validation # i.e. DBH, HIST), rows (10-24) identify standard validation rules with a number “01- or 02” which are referenced in the DESKTOP APPLICATION as (DBH01 or DBH02) and the red fonts are used to highlight where changes or updates can be made, and rows (25-34) identify Project Specific rules that needs to be developed.
Open the Validation Rules on the DESKTOP APPLICATION
The validation rules are developed in the DESKTOP APPLICATION. Open the application and select “Tools” then “Options”.
The ‘Options’ dialog box opens. Expand the “+” for CFI under ‘Categories’.
Then select “Validation” and the Validation Rules opens to the right. There are two types of rules “Single” and “Complex” rules.
Types of Validation Rules
Single Rules - There are single rules for Plot, Regen and Tree. Only add new field names to either check a valid code or specify a range. Single Rules only have a Parent Rule with no “Child Rules”.

Complex Rules - There are complex rules for Plot, Regen and Tree. Add or update the validation rules. Complex Rules has one Parent Rules and one or more Child Rules. In this example, the Parent Rule is “DBH02-IDBH: DBH Increasing Trend, and the Child Rule is “TreeHistory: Tree is Live, Missing, Ingrowth”. The error check work as follows: in the example: first the child rule Tree History must have these codes 0,5,10, in order to check the Parent Rule DBH should have an increasing trend that shouldn’t grow more than 2”. When a live tree’s dbh grows more than 2” then error will show in the Error Check report. However, if the live tree’s dbh grows less than 2” then there is no error.

Setup the “FieldDisplayRuleChecks”
The “FieldDisplayRuleChecks” table contain standard checks and will be updated to for the project.
ID = autogenerated numbers

RuleID: All the field names entered into the “FieldDisplayRules” table ‘CheckDescription’ is listed in the drop down. These will either be displayed or not in the tablet based on the “CheckColumnID” and the “CheckValue”.

CheckColumnID = All the field names from the AppColumn table with the “DisplayRule” checked are listed in the drop down. These are the main field names such as “TreeHistory” with “CheckValue” such as Live (0,5,10) and Mortality (2,3,7) will either turn on or off for the “RuleID”. CheckValue = are the codes for the “CheckColumnID” that will either display or not display the “RuleID”. For example, for ID 1 in the table below - when TreeHistory codes 0,5,10,2,3,7,1,4 is entered then MinorPlot will display for data entry in the tablet for only those TreeHistory values.

Develop Tally Sheets in C1 Report

The standard tally sheet is required for all CFI projects. Only certain past plot data should be provided on the tally sheets. The purpose is to make new field calls for current measurement observation without bias from past data e.g. Cover Type, Stockability, etc. The field unit should always have a backup in case the electronic data recorders fail. The goal is to ensure the continuity of field work.
Download the Application and Standard Tally Sheet
Establish a new folder “C1 Report” on the local C:Drive. The standard folder will be used to hold the standard C1 Report application, standard tally sheet file and the CFI project database.
Navigate to the Standardize CFI Materials Teams Channel/4. Tally Sheets and download both the “[Tribe] TallySheets [Date].flxr and the C1 Report Designer.zip.

Open 2 “File Explorer” folders. The 1st “File Explorer” open the “Download” folder, and the 2nd “File Explorer” open the new folder “C1 Report”. Copy the “[Tribe] TallySheets [Date].flxr” files and paste into the “C1 Report” folder.

Open all the folders in the “OneData_2_12-24-2023.zip” file in the “Downloads” of the 1st “Field Explorer” to the “C1 report Designer” folder. Use the Hot Key (CTRL+c) to copy the “C1 Report Designer” folder, then paste (CTRL+v). Close the “File Explorer” for “Downloads.”
Double click to open the “C1 Report Designer” folder and scroll down to find the “FlexReportDesigner32.4.8.exe”.
Note: Do not open or use “FlexReportDesigner4.8.exe”. If open the following error is received.
If the program is open for the first time the Windows protected your PC open – Select “More info” may display. The application will open automatically once it has been run for the first time.
Select “Run anyway” and keep n the “C1 Report” folder.
The Application screen opens and the for ComponentOne.

Update the Plot SQL to Show Past Data if Required to Added Field Names

Past data may be made available on the Plots tally sheet only. It is recommended that not all past data be made available. The foresters or contractors need to make the new call in the field based on the observations; providing past data may create bias.

Always copy and paste a field name that contains the boxes with an icon for “data source” and the past data. This is the simplest way to add new field names that requires the past data available. There are two types of Plot data entry boxes. The “FLC Commercial” box is just a data entry box, The “Habitat Type” and “Management Unit” boxes contain an icon “data source” in the upper right corner (red circle) that identifies a past data will be available.

Select “Query 1” and view in the “Design View”. From the “Plots”, “Plot Measurements” or “Plot Custom Measurements” table select the field name(s) that need to be added to the query add at the end after “RemarksPlot”. In this example added the “CutTreatment” (only as an example).
Select the “SQL” again for “Query 1” and copy the new “Sql statement” into the ComponentOne and select “OK”. Close “Query 1” without saving.
Select the icon “source data” box (arrow pointing) to review the Properties. In the Properties look for “Text” under Data.
Select the “ManagementUnit” box and a drop appears select “CutTreatment” and the icon box title changes.
